rulings.Case Title: KAISER KASABIAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Set Aside The Requst For Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC'S Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Set Aside The Request For Entry Of Default. The motion to set aside the default is granted. The Court will hear defendant's motion to quash on July 24. Plaintiff to file any opposition by July 18, defendant to file reply by July 22. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD EYE BLIND,INC VS. NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE CO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Rawson Dec. And Robbins Dec. Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 18. CROSS DEFENDANTS DAVID RAWSON and ZIESLER ZEISLER & RAWSON LLP'S Motion To File Rawson Dec. And Robbins Dec. Under Seal. The motion is granted as to exhibit G to the Rawson declaration only. It is denied as to the other documents for the reasons stated in connection with Provident's summary judgment motion. =(302/PJM/xMW)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD EYE BLIND,INC VS. NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE CO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Authorizing Filing Of Exhibits To Declaration Of R.W. Tarr Under Seal In Support Of Provident Financial Mgmt'S Opposition To Xdfs' Mtn For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 18. CROSS COMPLAINANT PROVIDENT FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT'S Motion For Order Authorizing Filing Of Exhibits To Declaration Of R.W. Tarr Under Seal In Support Of Provident Financial Management'S Opposition To Cross defendants' Motion For Good Faith Settlement. The motion is granted as to exhibits 9 and 23 to the Tarr declaration. It is denied as to the balance of the documents for the reasons stated in connection with Provident's summary judgment motion. =(302/PJM/xMW)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD EYE BLIND,INC VS. NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE CO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Authorizing Filing Of Exhibits To Declaration Of Ralph W. Tarr In Support Of Provident Financial Management'S Motion For Summary Judgment Etc. Under Seal.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 18. DEFENDANT PROVIDENT FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT'S Motion For Order Authorizing Filing Of Exhibits To Declaration Of Ralph W. Tarr In Support Of Provident Financial Management'S Motion For Summary Judgment Etc. Under Seal. The motion is denied for failure to meet the requirements of California Rule of Court 2.550 (d). There has been no showing that the insurance policies and related correspondence are confidential; the agreements at issue are 9 years old and there has been no showing that the agreements contain confidential or proprietary information. =(302/PJM/xMW)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES, USA, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Assuming defendant shifted the burden of proof, the Rivera declaration creates a triable issue of fact as to decedent's exposure to defendant's original equipment. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff has failed to present evidence linking decedent to products manufactured or supplied by defendant that contained asbestos. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA L. BROWN et al VS. CLAY HILL CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT CLAY HILL CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. The Court of Appeals directed the trial Court to hear the demurrer. The statute of limitations is 3 years for the negligence property claim and 4 years for the breach of fiduciary duty claim. The Court has considered the allegations of the 1st and 2nd amended complaints and finds that plaintiff has plead sufficient facts of the time and manner of discovery, due diligence and a lack of the requisite notice prior to September 2003. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDHIS ZADAFSHAR et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER SUTTER HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT MARYAM ARJOMAND'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. The Court is unable to determine, as a matter of law, that the deep burn was an undisclosed inherent complication with a low probability of occurrence. Piedra v. Dugan (2004) 123 Cal. App. 4th 1483, 1497. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN D. HOLLOMAN MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 15, 2008, line 16. PETITIONER JOHN HOLLOMAN'S MOTION For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus. The writ is denied. Petitioner has unreasonably delayed in seeking relief. Bus. & Prof. Code 2337. The Board had jurisdiction because the proceeding was based on the Michigan decision in May of 2003. The decision is supported by the evidence and the penalty is not excessive given the assessment that was made of petitioner's abilities in the Michigan proceeding. The Board did not violate petitioner's due process rights for there was no obligation to retry the Michigan case. Marek v. Board of Podiatric Medicine (1993) 16 Cal App. 4th 1089, 1097-1098. =(302/PJM/VC)
